galadriel: She was the "Lady" of the woods of Lothlórien, which she ruled with Celeborn her husband.
gilGalad: He was the last High King of the Ñoldor in Middle-earth.
feanor: Fëanor was a Ñoldorin Elf, first King of the Noldor.
celebrimbor: Celebrimbor was a Ñoldorin prince and the last in the line of the House of Fëanor, who lived in Middle-earth
noldor: The Ñoldor were the second clan of those Elves who joined the Great March and came to the West.
fingolfin: Fingolfin was the first High King of the Ñoldor in Beleriand.
finrod: Finrod was a ÑoldorinElf who was born in Aman, He was King of Nargothrond and the eldest son of Finarfin, and the brother of Galadriel.
finwe: Finwë was the first High King of the Ñoldor who led his people on the journey from Middle-earth to Valinor in the blessed realm of Aman.
aegnor: Aegnor was a Ñoldorin Elf, the third son of Finarfin, elder brother of Galadriel.
anaire: Anairë was an Elf of Valinor and the wife of Fingolfin.
angrod: Angrod was a Ñoldorin Elf, the second son of Finarfin and lord of the Ñoldor of Dorthonion.
aredhel: Aredhel, the "White Lady of the Ñoldor", was the only daughter and third child of the Noldorin High-prince Fingolfin.
argon: Argon was the fourth child and the youngest son of Fingolfin, the High King of the Ñoldor in Beleriand.
caranthir: Caranthir, also known as Caranthir the Dark, was the fourth son of Fëanor.
celegorm: Celegorm, called Celegorm the Fair, was the third son of Fëanor and Nerdanel, most closely associated with another brother, Curufin.
curufin: Curufin, also called Curufin the Crafty, was a Ñoldorin prince and the fifth of the seven Sons of Fëanor and Nerdanel.
ecthelion: Ecthelion, or Ecthelion of the Fountain, was a mighty lord of Gondolin in the First Age of Middle-earth, and one of its greatest heroes.
egalmoth: Egalmoth was an Ñoldorin Elf-lord of Gondolin in the First Age.
eldalote: Eldalótë was the wife of Angrod, second son of Finarfin, and grandmother of Finduilas and Gil-galad.
elenwe: Elenwë was a Vanyarin elf, the wife of Turgon and the mother of Idril.
finarfin: Finarfin was a Ñoldorin Elf of Valinor.
finduilas: Finduilas was a Ñoldor Elf princess of the First Age, daughter of Orodreth, ruler of the hidden realm of Nargothrond.
fingon: Fingon the "Valiant" was an Ñoldorin Elf, the eldest son of Fingolfin, and older brother of Turgon, Aredhel, and Argon.
gildor: Gildor Inglorion was a Ñoldor Elf met by Frodo Baggins on the edge of the Shire, at the beginning of Frodo's venture out of his home.
glorfindel: Glorfindel was among the mightiest of the Elves, and was once the lord of the House of the Golden Flower in Gondolin.
houseOfFinarfin: The House of Finarfin was a noble house of Ñoldorin Elves.
houseOfFingolfin: The House of Fingolfin was a royal House of the Ñoldor.
houseOfFinwe: The House of Finwë was the founding royal house of the Ñoldor in Arda.
houseOfFeanor: The House of Fëanor was a noble house of the Ñoldor Elves.
idril: Idril, also called Celebrindal, was a Ñoldorin Elf of Gondolin. She was the daughter of Turgon and Elenwë, wife of the Man Tuor.
irime: Írimë was a Ñoldorin Elf, the younger daughter of Finwë, High King of the Ñoldor, and Indis.
maedhros: Maedhros, also called Maedhros the Tall, was one of the princes of the Ñoldor, the eldest of the seven Sons of Fëanor.
maeglin: Maeglin was an Elf, the son of Eöl the "Dark Elf" and Aredhel, daughter of Fingolfin.
maglor: Maglor was the second son of Fëanor and Nerdanel. He was the greatest poet and minstrel of the Ñoldor.
mahtan: Mahtan was a renowned Ñoldorin metallurgist of Aman and the father of Nerdanel, the wife of Fëanor.
miriel: Míriel Therindë was the first wife of Finwë, the High King of the Ñoldor and the mother of Fëanor.
nerdanel: Nerdanel, was the daughter of the Ñoldorin smith Mahtan and the wife of the infamous Fëanor, eldest son of King Finwë.
orodreth: Orodreth was a Ñoldorin Elf of the First Age who was the second ruler of the underground city of Nargothrond in western Beleriand.
rumilOfTirion: Rúmil was a knowledgeable loremaster of the Ñoldor living in the city of Tirion, in Valinor.
salgant: Salgant was an Elf of Gondolin.
sonsOfFeanor: The seven Sons of Fëanor, the great Ñoldorin King, led their people from Valinor to rule over kingdoms in the Northeast of Beleriand.
turgon: Turgon was a Ñoldorin Elf of Gondolin, second son of Fingolfin, brother of Fingon, Aredhel, Argon, and father of Idril.
voronwe: Voronwë, also called Aranwion, was a Ñoldorin Elf mariner from Gondolin who was one of a crew of several ships sent by Turgon to solicit aid from the Valar.
vanyar: The Vanyar, also known as the Fair-elves or Light-elves, were the first and smallest of Three Kindreds of the Eldar.
amarie: Amarië was a Vanyar Elf of Valinor in Aman.
indis: Indis a Vanyarin Elf-maiden of Valinor and second wife of Finwë, King of the Ñoldor.
ingwion: Ingwion was a Vanyarin Elf and the son of Ingwë, High King of the Elves.
ingwe: Ingwë was the Vanyarin Elf, who held titles of the King of the Vanyar and High King of the Elves.
falmari: The Falmari, also known as the Sea-elves, were those of the Teleri who departed from Middle-earth.
earwen: Eärwen was the daughter of Olwë and had several unnamed brothers. She was the wife of Finarfin, and mother of Finrod, Angrod, Aegnor, and Galadriel.
olwe: Olwë was an Elf of the Teleri of Aman.
beleg: Beleg was a Sindarin Elf who served in the army of King Elu Thingol of Doriath as Chief Marchwarden.
celeborn: Celeborn was the Lord of Lothlórien, and the husband of Galadriel, Lady of the Golden Wood.
daeron: Daeron was a Sindarin elf of Doriath and King Thingol's loremaster and minstrel.
elwing: Elwing also known as Elwing the White, was the daughter of Dior the Fair and his wife Nimloth.
dior: Dior was the son of Beren Erchamion of the House of Bëor and the princess Lúthien Tinúviel, daughter of King Thingol.
thingol: Elu Thingol was an Elf of the Teleri during the First Age.
luthien: Lúthien was an Elf-maiden of Doriath, the wife of Beren Erchamion, and the most beautiful of all the Children of Ilúvatar to ever live.
mablung: Mablung was a Sindar Elf who served in the army of King Elu Thingol of Doriath as a captain and Marchwarden.
nellas: Nellas was an Elf-maiden of Doriath during the First Age.
oropher: Oropher was the Sindar King of the Silvan Elves of Greenwood the Great during the Second Age.
thranduil: Thranduil, son of Oropher, was an Elven king who ruled over the Woodland Realm in the Third Age.
teleri: The Teleri; meaning Those who come last, were the third of the Elf clans who came to Aman.
avari: The Avari were a branch of the Elven race in Middle-earth, the group of Moriquendi ('Elves of Darkness') who never journeyed to Aman.
eol: Eöl, always called the Dark Elf, was an Elf of Beleriand and a metallurgist of high skill.
galadhrim: The Galadhrim were the Elven people of mixed Nandorin, Avarin, and Sindarin descent, who lived in the forest of Lothlórien.
amroth: Amroth was an Elven king of Lothlórien.
celebrian: Celebrían was an Elven noblewoman, the daughter of Celeborn and Galadriel, wife of Elrond, and the mother of Elladan, Elrohir and Arwen.
cirdan: Círdan, also known as Nowë and Círdan the Shipwright, was one of the highest and most noble of the Sindar.
haldir: Haldir was an Elf of Lothlórien, probably a Silvan Elf, and a marchwarden who guarded the forest's northern borders.
nimrodel: Nimrodel was a Nandorin Elf-maiden of Lórien in the Third Age.
orophin: Orophin was a Silvan Marchwarden from Lothlórien, and the brother of Haldir and Rúmil.
rumilOfLorien: Rúmil was an Elf of Lórien and brother of Orophin and Haldir, the leading Marchwarden of Lórien, during the Third Age.
galdor: Galdor was an Elf of the Grey Havens.
legolas: Legolas was a Sindar Elf who was part of the Fellowship of the Ring in the Third Age.
sindar: The Sindar, also known as the Grey-elves, were a sub-group of the Telerin Elves, who chose to stay in Beleriand.
nandor: The Nandor were Elves of Telerin descent, who left the Great Journey from Cuiviénen to Valinor as the Elves reached the Misty Mountains.
denethor: Denethor was the Nandorin Elf and son of Lenwë.
galion: Galion was a Nandorin Wood-elf of the Woodland Realm in northern Mirkwood.
laiquendi: The Laiquendi, also known as the Green-elves, were a group of Nandorin Elves under Denethor, son of Lenwë.
silvanElves: The Silvan Elves were a subgroup of the Nandor, ultimately of Telerin descent.
arwen: Arwen was the Half-elven daughter of Elrond and Celebrían. She was often called Arwen Undómiel or "Evenstar".
elladanAndElrohir: Elladan and Elrohir were the twin sons of Elrond the Half-elven and Celebrían of Rivendell.
elrond: Elrond Lord of Rivendell, was a mighty Elf-ruler of old who dwelt in Middle-earth from the First Age to the beginning of the Fourth Age.
erestor: Erestor was an Elf of Rivendell and a chief of Elrond's household.
lindir: Lindir was an Elf of unknown kindred residing in Rivendell when Frodo met Bilbo there in TA 3018.
frodoBaggins: Frodo Baggins, son of Drogo Baggins, was a hobbit of the Shire in the late Third Age.
deagol: Déagol was the cousin and best friend of Sméagol until his discovery of the Ring.
samwiseGamgee: Samwise Gamgee, known as Sam, was a hobbit of the Shire. He was Frodo Baggins' gardener and best friend.
gollum: Gollum, originally named Sméagol (or Trahald), was at first a Stoor, one of the three early Hobbit-types.
isildur: Isildur was the oldest son of Elendil and brother to Anárion.
sauron: Sauron, the eponymous Lord of the Rings, was the greatest subversive Maia, the maker of the One Ring.
durinIII: Durin III was a King of Durin's Folk, and the first bearer of the mightiest of the seven Dwarf Rings.
durinIV: Durin IV was a King of Durin's Folk and of Khazad-dûm in the Second Age.
durinVI: Durin VI was the sixth Dwarven king to bear the name of Durin.
dainI: Dáin I was a Dwarf and a King of Durin's Folk in the Third Age. He was the son of Náin II and had a younger brother, Borin.
gandalf: Gandalf originally named Olórin, was an Istar (Wizard), dispatched to Middle-earth in the Third Age to combat the threat of Sauron.
khamul: Khamûl was one of the nine Ringwraiths second to the Witch-king, in Middle-earth.
nazgul: The Nazgûl were the dreaded ring-servants of the Dark Lord Sauron in Middle-earth throughout the Second and Third Ages.
morgulKnife: The Morgul-knife was a short blade carried by the Witch-king of Angmar during the Third Age.
earnur: Eärnur was the thirty-third and last King of Gondor until the accession of Aragorn II Elessar.
helmHammerhand: Helm Hammerhand was the ninth King of Rohan, and the last of the first line of its Kings.
witchKing: The Witch-king of Angmar, also known as the Lord of the Nazgûl, was the leader of the Nazgûl (Ringwraiths).
wraithWorld: The wraith-world was the unseen realm in which cursed spirits such as the Ringwraiths had form.
wraiths: A wraith is someone who has been killed yet lives on by some dark sorcery (necromancy or a Morgul-blade).
nainI: Náin I was the son of Durin VI, and succeeded his father as King of Khazad-dûm.
thrainII: Thráin II was the father of Thorin II Oakenshield, and the son of King Thrór, making him a direct descendant of Durin the Deathless.
thror: Thrór I was King of Durin's Folk, the son of Dáin I and brother of Frór and Grór, known for ruling Erebor in the Lonely Mountain.
elves: The Elves, were the first and eldest of the Children of Ilúvatar, considered the fairest and wisest of the earthly race of Arda.
amrod: Amrod was the youngest son of Fëanor, along with his twin brother Amras.
cuivienen: Cuiviénen was the location where the Quendi (or Elves) awoke.
dorthonion: Dorthonion, later Taur-nu-Fuin, was a highland region of the First Age lying immediately to the north of Beleriand.
elros: Elros, the Half-elven, was the son of Eärendil and Elwing, brother of Elrond, and the first King of Númenor.
eluredAndElurin: Eluréd and Elurín were the twin sons of Dior Eluchîl, son of Beren and Lúthien, and Dior's Elven wife, Nimloth.
earendil: Eärendil was a great Half-elven mariner who voyaged to Valinor.
houseOfFinrod: The House of Finrod no longer has a place that it can call home, and is mostly made up of wanderers.
houseOfThingol: The House of Thingol was a noble house of Sindar Elves.
melian: Melian was an Ainu of the race of the Maiar of Yavanna, wife of King Elu Thingol, Queen of Doriath, and mother of Lúthien Tinúviel.
nienna: Nienna was an Ainu, one of the Aratar and a Vala who was responsible for the mercy and grief spread across Arda.
thorinII: Thorin II "Oakenshield", eventually called King under the Mountain or Mountain King, was the son of Thráin II, the older brother of Frerin
bilboBaggins: Bilbo Baggins was a hobbit of the Shire, the main protagonist of The Hobbit and a secondary character in The Lord of the Rings.
gloin: Glóin, son of Gróin was one of the Dwarves of Thorin II Oakenshield's company who set out to reclaim the Lonely Mountain in the Quest of Erebor.
saruman: Saruman, was first of the order of Istari (Wizards), who came to Middle-earth as emissaries of the Valar in the Third Age.
aragorn: Aragorn II, son of Arathorn II and Gilraen, also known as Elessar and Strider, was the 16th and last Chieftain of the Dúnedain of the North
fellbeast: The term "Fellbeast" refers to the flying creatures that the Nazgûl rode after being unhorsed at the Ford of Bruinen, in The Lord of the Rings.
wargs: Wargs were a breed of wolves who lived in the Misty Mountains.
mordor: Mordor was a black, volcanic plain in the southeast of Middle-earth to the east of Gondor, Ithilien, and the great river Anduin.
gondor: Gondor was the most prominent kingdom of Men in Middle-earth.
radagast: Radagast the Brown was one of five Wizards sent to Middle-earth to contest the will of Sauron.
blueWizards: The Blue Wizards were two mysterious characters of Middle-earth, named as such because they both wore sea-blue robes.
